description: Creates a managed agent configuration by calling the anthropic-create-agent API. Requires model, system prompt, and optionally tools and request_id. Returns agent details upon success.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: To create a managed agent, send a POST request to /v1/agents with the following JSON body: {"model": "<model>", "system_prompt": "<system_prompt>", "tools": <tools>} and optionally include a request_id header. Parse the response to obtain the agent ID.

model.description: The model identifier for the agent (e.g., claude-3-opus-20240229).

request_id.description: Optional request ID header for idempotency.

system_prompt.description: The system prompt that defines the agent's behavior.

tools.description: List of tool definitions the agent can use.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the created agent.

postconditions: A new agent configuration is created on the server.
The agent is ready to be used with the specified model, prompt, and tools.
preconditions: API server is reachable and accepting requests.
User has valid authentication credentials if required.

name: create_managed_agent_api_doc_anthropic_create_agent

challenges.expected_evidence: A successful HTTP response (e.g., 200 or 201) with a response body containing agent details such as an agent ID.
challenges.failure_signal: HTTP error status (e.g., 400, 422) indicating missing parameters or invalid format.
rubric: Correctly uses the POST method and endpoint path /v1/agents.
Includes required fields: model, system_prompt, tools.
Parses the response to confirm agent creation.
challenges.task: Given a model name, system prompt, and a list of tools, create a managed agent using the provided API.

challenges.expected_evidence: Either a successful creation response (if tools are optional) or a clear error indicating that tools are required.
challenges.failure_signal: Unexpected error or failure to send a valid request.
rubric: Omits the tools field from the JSON body.
Still uses correct endpoint and method.
Observes whether the API returns an error due to missing tools (if required) or succeeds.
challenges.task: Create a managed agent with only the model and system prompt, omitting tools, and verify the request is accepted.

challenges.expected_evidence: A 4xx or 5xx error with a message indicating the system prompt is too long or the request is malformed.
challenges.failure_signal: Silent truncation or unexpected success without validation.
rubric: Includes a system_prompt field with a string longer than 10,000 characters.
Interprets the status code and error message if rejected.
challenges.task: Create an agent with a very long system prompt (exceeding typical limits) and observe the API's response.

constraints: The request must be sent to the server URL https://example.invalid (placeholder).
The endpoint only supports POST method.
The request_id header is optional but may be used for idempotency or tracking.
examples: Example request: POST /v1/agents with body {"model": "claude-3-opus-20240229", "system_prompt": "You are a helpful assistant.", "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_weather", "description": "Get weather for a location", "parameters": {"type": "object", "properties": {"location": {"type": "string"}}}}} ]}
facts: The summary states: Create a managed agent configuration with model, system prompt, and tools.
A request_id header parameter is accepted.
procedures: To create an agent, send a POST request to /v1/agents with a JSON body containing model, system_prompt, and tools fields, and optionally include a request_id header.
context_pack.summary: REST API endpoint to create a managed agent configuration by specifying a model, system prompt, and tools, with an optional request ID header.

ctx2skill_evidence.demo_scope: P0/P1 demo-paper implementation; not full long-horizon self-play training.

judge_results.rationale: Ctx2Skill-lite replay score meets the demo threshold.

ctx2skill_evidence.selected_reason: The candidate 'create_managed_agent' directly implements the single API operation described in the context, covering all required inputs and expected outputs. It is atomic, reusable, and addresses the challenge tasks reliably. No other candidate was needed since the context only describes one endpoint. The score reflects high confidence due to precise mapping between the API summary and the skill definition.
